events.course_title: Gramsci for Aotearoa
events.title: Lesson 1: Who was Gramsci, and why read him now?
events.teaser: Introduction to Gramsci's life, ideas, and relevance today.

events.course_title: Gramsci for Aotearoa
events.title: Lesson 2: Hegemony — how ruling ideas become ordinary life
events.teaser: Why ruling ideas become ordinary life.

events.course_title: Gramsci for Aotearoa
events.title: Lesson 3: Organic Intellectuals — who speaks for the working class?
events.teaser: Who speaks for the working class and how?

events.course_title: Gramsci for Aotearoa
events.title: Lesson 4: Civil Society, the State, and who controls the institutions
events.teaser: How institutions reproduce power — and how to fight back.

events.course_title: Gramsci for Aotearoa
events.title: Lesson 5: War of Position — building counter-power from below
events.teaser: The long game of building socialist counter-power.

events.course_title: Gramsci for Aotearoa
events.title: Lesson 6: Putting it all together — a socialist toolkit for Aotearoa
events.teaser: Applying Gramsci's framework to Aotearoa New Zealand today.

events.course_title: Kropotkin & Mutual Aid
events.title: Lesson 1: Who was Kropotkin, and what is mutual aid?
events.teaser: An introduction to cooperation beyond capitalist myth-making.

events.course_title: Kropotkin & Mutual Aid
events.title: Course intro — free email course from The Kiwi Dialect
events.teaser: Overview and how to enrol.

events.course_title: Kropotkin & Mutual Aid
events.title: Lesson 2: Mutual Aid vs Social Darwinism — nature is not a war of all against all
events.teaser: The idea that humans are naturally selfish is ideology, not science.

events.course_title: Kropotkin & Mutual Aid
events.title: Lesson 3: Mutual Aid in Working-Class History — strikes, friendly societies, and solidarity networks
events.teaser: Working-class people have always built mutual aid networks to survive capitalism.

events.course_title: Kropotkin & Mutual Aid
events.title: Lesson 4: Mutual Aid in Aotearoa — hapū, whānau, working-class communities
events.teaser: Mutual aid is not a Western import to Aotearoa. It was already here.

events.course_title: Kropotkin & Mutual Aid
events.title: Lesson 5: The State vs Mutual Aid — when welfare replaced self-organised solidarity
events.teaser: The welfare state was a genuine gain — and also used to dismantle self-organisation.

events.course_title: Kropotkin & Mutual Aid
events.title: Lesson 6: Mutual Aid Now — from food banks to tenants unions to climate response
events.teaser: Mutual aid is not theoretical. It is happening right now.

events.course_title: Graeber: Debt, Bullshit Jobs & Direct Democracy
events.title: Course intro — free email course from The Kiwi Dialect
events.teaser: Six lessons on Graeber's big ideas — free. Enrol here.

events.course_title: Gilles Deleuze for Educators, Artists, and Organisers
events.title: Course intro — coming soon
events.teaser: Overview of the upcoming Deleuze course.

events.course_title: He Ako mo ngā Umanga Mahi Ngātahi — How to Start a Cooperative in Aotearoa
events.title: Course intro — He Ako mo ngā Umanga Mahi Ngātahi
events.teaser: How to start a cooperative in Aotearoa — overview and how to follow along.

events.course_title: Graeber: Debt, Bullshit Jobs & Direct Democracy
events.title: Lesson 1: Who was Graeber, and why does anthropology make you dangerous?
events.teaser: Anthropology, anarchism, and seeing the world differently.

events.course_title: Graeber: Debt, Bullshit Jobs & Direct Democracy
events.title: Lesson 2: Debt — the first 5,000 years and why you should never feel morally obligated to a bank
events.teaser: How debt was invented as a tool of domination long before capitalism.

events.course_title: Graeber: Debt, Bullshit Jobs & Direct Democracy
events.title: Lesson 3: Bullshit Jobs — why so much work under capitalism is meaningless by design
events.teaser: Why most work under capitalism is pointless by design.

events.course_title: Graeber: Debt, Bullshit Jobs & Direct Democracy
events.title: Lesson 4: The Utopia of Rules — why bureaucracy is a problem for the left, not just the right
events.teaser: How paperwork, means-testing, and administration humiliate and exhaust people.

events.course_title: Graeber: Debt, Bullshit Jobs & Direct Democracy
events.title: Lesson 5: The Dawn of Everything — what the deep past tells us about human freedom
events.teaser: What Occupy Wall Street and other movements actually built.

events.course_title: Graeber: Debt, Bullshit Jobs & Direct Democracy
events.title: Lesson 6: Synthesis — what Graeber's work means for thinking and acting in Aotearoa today
events.teaser: Debt, work, bureaucracy, and direct democracy through a New Zealand lens.

events.course_title: Tangi o te Tāheke — Bakunin mō Aotearoa
events.title: Course launch — Tangi o te Tāheke
events.teaser: The cry of the waterfall. Why Bakunin, why Aotearoa, why now — and how this course closes the AI Warrior thinkers series.

events.course_title: Tangi o te Tāheke — Bakunin mō Aotearoa
events.title: Lesson 1: Ko wai a Bakunin? — the man Marx feared
events.teaser: The Russian who split the First International, defended the Paris Commune, and warned that any state — even a workers' state — becomes a new prison.

events.course_title: Tangi o te Tāheke — Bakunin mō Aotearoa
events.title: Lesson 2: Mana motuhake — federation from below, not the state from above
events.teaser: Bakunin's free federation read alongside He Whakaputanga and tino rangatiratanga. Power binds upward from the hapū, never downward from Wellington.

events.course_title: Tangi o te Tāheke — Bakunin mō Aotearoa
events.title: Lesson 3: Te tāheke — the passion for destruction is also a creative passion
events.teaser: How to tear down without nihilism. The waterfall as kaupapa: what falls feeds the awa, what is demolished irrigates the kāinga.

events.course_title: Tangi o te Tāheke — Bakunin mō Aotearoa
events.title: Lesson 4: Caracoles in the kāinga — a Zapatista reading for Aotearoa
events.teaser: Chiapas to Ōtepoti. Autonomous councils, mandar obedeciendo, and what a Kiwi caracol looks like — marae, co-ops, learning circles, mutual-aid pātaka.

events.course_title: Tangi o te Tāheke — Bakunin mō Aotearoa
events.title: Lesson 5: Refusing the algorithmic Leviathan — anarchism in the age of AI
events.teaser: Bakunin called the state a machine. The machine is now literal. How federation, encryption, and refusal apply to platform capital and state AI.

events.course_title: Tangi o te Tāheke — Bakunin mō Aotearoa
events.title: Lesson 6: Arm the class — closing the AI Warrior thinkers series
events.teaser: From Gramsci to Bakunin in six thinkers. The toolkit, the refrain, the kaupapa for the next decade. E kore e mau te rongo ki te whenua kāore e watea.
